description: Poverty indicators for Nigeria — multiple measures from different sources

sources.name: Finance in Africa / World Bank projections

note: Poverty data uses multiple definitions. International poverty line ($2.15/day), national poverty line, and multidimensional poverty index (MPI) all tell different stories. Survey data is sparse — estimates between survey years are World Bank modeled projections.

internationalPovertyLine.description: Poverty headcount ratio at $3.00 a day (2021 PPP) (% of population) — World Bank

nationalPovertyLine.description: Poverty rate at national poverty line — World Bank estimates and projections

data.note: World Bank projection — consumption fell 6.7% 2019-2023, ultra-poor rose from 14% to 27% (~70M people)
World Bank projection — estimated 139 million Nigerians below poverty line
World Bank projection — expected peak before modest decline
World Bank projection — first predicted reversal in nearly a decade

multidimensionalPoverty.description: NBS Multidimensional Poverty Index (2022) — measures deprivation across health, education, and living standards

keyInsight: Food inflation is the single biggest driver of poverty in Nigeria — staple food prices consumed by the poor rose fivefold between 2020 and 2024 (World Bank).
